title: Variables 6
description: Rust has constants declared with `const`. Unlike `let` bindings, constants must have a type annotation and their value must be a constant expression.
instructions: Fix the constant declaration so the code compiles.
hints: Constants in Rust require an explicit type annotation.
The syntax for constants is: const NAME: Type = value;
Add a type annotation like `: i32` after the constant name.
explanation: Constants in Rust are declared with `const` and always require a type annotation. They differ from `let` bindings in several ways: they can be declared in any scope including global, they must be set to a constant expression (computed at compile time), and they are always immutable - you cannot use `mut` with constants.
